# frozen_string_literal: true

class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
  include HasUrl
  belongs_to :upload

  # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
  VERSION = 2
  URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}

  def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
    @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
    # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
    # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
    #
    # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
    if lock_per_machine?
      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
        DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
      end
    else
      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    end
  end

  def self.lock_per_machine?
    return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
    @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
  end

  def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
    @lock_per_machine = value
  end

  def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
    return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
    return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?

    # no extension so try to guess it
    upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)

    if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
      if opts[:raise_on_error]
        raise InvalidAccess
      else
        # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
        return
      end
    end

    # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
    extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
    thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)

    # correct bad thumbnail if needed
    if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
      thumbnail.destroy!
      thumbnail = nil
    end

    return thumbnail if thumbnail

    store = Discourse.store

    # create the thumbnail otherwise
    original_path = store.path_for(upload)

    if original_path.blank?
      # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
      original_path = store.download(upload)
    end

    if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
      if opts[:raise_on_error]
        raise InvalidAccess
      else
        # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
        return
      end
    end

    lock(upload.id, width, height) do
      # may have been generated since we got the lock
      thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)

      # return the previous thumbnail if any
      return thumbnail if thumbnail

      if original_path.blank?
        Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
      else
        # create a temp file with the same extension as the original

        return nil if extension.length == 1

        temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
        temp_path = temp_file.path

        target_quality =
          upload.target_image_quality(
            original_path,
            SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
          )
        opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
        opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)

        # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
        if extension == ".svg"
          FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
          resized = true
        elsif opts[:crop]
          resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
        else
          resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
        end

        if resized
          # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
          thumbnail =
            OptimizedImage.create!(

# frozen_string_literal: true

##
# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
#
# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
# option is present.
#
# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
# complex queries and joins to perform.
#
# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
#
# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
class UploadSecurity
  @@custom_public_types = []

  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
    avatar
    custom_emoji
    profile_background
    card_background
    category_logo
    category_logo_dark
    category_background
    group_flair
    badge_image
    site_setting
  ]

  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
    Badge
    Category
    CustomEmoji
    Group
    SiteSetting
    ThemeField
    User
    UserAvatar
    UserProfile
  ]

  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
  end

  # used in tests
  def self.reset_custom_public_types
    @@custom_public_types = []
  end

  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
    @upload = upload
    @opts = opts
    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
    @creating = @opts[:creating]
  end

  def should_be_secure?
    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
  end

  def should_be_secure_with_reason
    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
    end

    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
  end

  private

  def access_control_post
    @access_control_post ||=
      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
  end

  def insecure_context_checks
    {
      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
      public_type: "upload is public type",
      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
    }
  end

  def secure_context_checks
    {
      login_required: "login is required",
      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
    }
  end

  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
  # immediately if there is an access control post.
  def priority_check?(check)
    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
  end

  def perform_check(check)
    send("#{check}_check")
  end

  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####

  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
  end

  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
    return false if !@creating
    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
  end

  def public_type_check
    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
  end

  def publicly_referenced_first_check
    return false if @creating
    first_reference =
      @upload
        .upload_references
        .joins(<<~SQL)
          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
        SQL
        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
        .first
    return false if first_reference.blank?
    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
  end

  def regular_emoji_check
    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
  end

  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####

  #--------------------------#

  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####

  def login_required_check
    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
  end

  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
  #
  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
  #
  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
  #
  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
  end

  def uploading_in_composer_check
    @upload_type == "composer"
  end

  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
    return false if !@creating
    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
  end

  def already_secure_check
    @upload.secure?
  end

  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
end

4	  GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/}
5	  LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper"
6	  MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100
7	  MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100
8	
9	  def post_process_oneboxes
10	    limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size
11	    oneboxes = {}
12	    inlineOneboxes = {}
13	
14	    Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element|
15	      is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS
16	      map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes
17	      skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url]
18	
19	      if skip_onebox
20	        if is_onebox
21	          element.remove_class("onebox")
22	        else
23	          remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element)
24	        end
25	
26	        next
27	      end
28

# frozen_string_literal: true

module CookedProcessorMixin
  GIF_SOURCES_REGEXP = %r{(giphy|tenor)\.com/}
  LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS = "lightbox-wrapper"
  MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH = 100
  MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT = 100

  def post_process_oneboxes
    limit = SiteSetting.max_oneboxes_per_post - @doc.css("aside.onebox, a.inline-onebox").size
    oneboxes = {}
    inlineOneboxes = {}

    Oneboxer.apply(@doc, extra_paths: [".inline-onebox-loading"]) do |url, element|
      is_onebox = element["class"] == Oneboxer::ONEBOX_CSS_CLASS
      map = is_onebox ? oneboxes : inlineOneboxes
      skip_onebox = limit <= 0 && !map[url]

      if skip_onebox
        if is_onebox
          element.remove_class("onebox")
        else
          remove_inline_onebox_loading_class(element)
        end

        next
      end

      limit -= 1
      map[url] = true

      if is_onebox
        onebox =
          Oneboxer.onebox(
            url,
            invalidate_oneboxes: !!@opts[:invalidate_oneboxes],
            user_id: @model&.user_id,
            category_id: @category_id,
          )

        @has_oneboxes = true if onebox.present?
        onebox
      else
        process_inline_onebox(element)
        false
      end
    end

    PrettyText.sanitize_hotlinked_media(@doc)

    oneboxed_images.each do |img|
      next if img["src"].blank?

      parent = img.parent

      if respond_to?(:process_hotlinked_image, true)
        still_an_image = process_hotlinked_image(img)
        next if !still_an_image
      end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
  include ActiveSupport::Concern

  def upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
    sha1 = File.basename(path_with_ext, File.extname(path_with_ext))
    # this takes care of optimized image requests
    sha1 = sha1.partition("_").first if sha1.include?("_")
    Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
  end

  def upload_from_full_url(url)
    Upload.find_by(sha1: Upload.sha1_from_long_url(url))
  end

  def check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
    if upload.access_control_post_id.present?
      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if current_user.nil? && SiteSetting.login_required
      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess if !guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)
    else
      raise Discourse::NotFound if current_user.nil?
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module GlobalPath
  def path(p)
    "#{GlobalSetting.relative_url_root}#{p}"
  end

  def cdn_path(p)
    GlobalSetting.cdn_url.blank? ? p : "#{GlobalSetting.cdn_url}#{path(p)}"
  end

  def upload_cdn_path(p)
    p = Discourse.store.cdn_url(p) if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.present?

    (p =~ /\Ahttp/ || p =~ %r{\A//}) ? p : cdn_path(p)
  end

  def cdn_relative_path(path)
    if (cdn_url = GlobalSetting.cdn_url).present?
      URI.parse(cdn_url).path + path
    else
      path
    end
  end

  def full_cdn_url(url)
    uri = URI.parse(UrlHelper.absolute(upload_cdn_path(url)))
    uri.scheme = SiteSetting.scheme if uri.scheme.blank?
    uri.to_s
  end

  extend self
end

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Here is the full audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Discourse — a large open-source forum platform. Rails monolith backend (`app/`, `lib/`) with an Ember.js frontend (`frontend/discourse/app`), Sidekiq background jobs (`app/jobs`), and a plugin system (`plugins/`).

**Layout that matters for this ticket:**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models, including the upload contract.
- `app/models/concerns/` — shared mixins included into models.
- `lib/file_store/` — the storage abstraction (local vs S3).
- `lib/` — service-like helpers (`upload_creator.rb`, `upload_security.rb`, `upload_recovery.rb`, `cooked_post_processor.rb`, `s3_inventory.rb`).
- `app/jobs/regular` + `app/jobs/scheduled` — async processing/cleanup.
- `lib/backup_restore/` — backup + restore.
- `app/services/` — service objects.

**The model representing an uploaded file:** `app/models/upload.rb:5` (`class Upload < ActiveRecord::Base`). The DB schema is annotated at `app/models/upload.rb:695-736` (key columns: `url`, `sha1`, `original_sha1`, `extension`, `secure`, `access_control_post_id`, `verification_status`).

**Shared pieces it is built on:**
- Concern `HasUrl` — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` (URL→record resolution by sha1/url).
- Join model `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` (polymorphic glue between an upload and posts/themes/etc.).
- Concern `HasPostUploadReferences` — `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:3` (extracts upload URLs from cooked HTML and links them).
- Storage abstraction `FileStore::BaseStore` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7`, with `LocalStore` (`lib/file_store/local_store.rb`) and `S3Store` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb`), reached via `Discourse.store`.
- Derived records: `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`) and `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb`).

---

## Step 2 — The Upload contract and what it is built on

**The model:** `app/models/upload.rb:5`.

**Shared behavior it pulls in:**
- `include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper` — `upload.rb:6` (used by `human_filesize`, `upload.rb:484`).
- `include HasUrl` — `upload.rb:7` → concern at `concerns/has_url.rb:3`. Provides class methods `extract_url`, `extract_sha1`, `get_from_url`, `get_from_urls` keyed off `URL_REGEX` (`upload.rb:11`). This is the contract other code uses to turn a URL/path back into an Upload.

**Associations other records hang off it:**
- `belongs_to :user` — `upload.rb:17`; `belongs_to :access_control_post` — `upload.rb:18` (overridden unscoped at `upload.rb:22` so deleted posts still resolve — security-critical).
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media` — `upload.rb:26`.
- `has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy` — `upload.rb:27` (variants).
- `has_many :optimized_videos` + `:optimized_video_uploads` through them — `upload.rb:28-29` (variants).
- `has_many :user_uploads` — `upload.rb:30`.
- `has_many :upload_references` + `has_many :posts, through:` — `upload.rb:31-32` (the polymorphic backbone).
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails` — `upload.rb:33`.
- `has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify` — `upload.rb:34`.

**Variant/derived spawning:**
- `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` — `upload.rb:35,690` → enqueues `Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, ...)`.
- `get_optimized_image` / `create_thumbnail!` — `upload.rb:147-172` → `OptimizedImage.create_for`.

**Teardown contract (the part being reworked):**
- `before_destroy` nulls `UserProfile.card_background_upload_id`/`profile_background_upload_id` — `upload.rb:53-58`.
- `after_destroy` nulls `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar.gravatar_upload_id`/`custom_upload_id` — `upload.rb:60-64`.
- `destroy` override wraps `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` + `super` in a transaction — `upload.rb:212-217`. **This is the teardown seam.**

**URL/identity contract surface a teammate must know:**
- `short_url` / `short_path` / `base62_sha1` — `upload.rb:219-289`.
- Secure-URL resolution: `secure_uploads_url?`, `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url`, `secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url` — `upload.rb:248-275`.
- sha1 extraction helpers: `sha1_from_short_path`/`short_url`/`long_url`/`base62_encoded` — `upload.rb:457-478`.
- `update_secure_status` + `sync_optimized_videos_secure_status` — `upload.rb:492-523` (mutates ACL on the store and cascades to optimized videos).
- `fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` — `upload.rb:86` (canonical lookup entry point).
- Verification status enum — `upload.rb:76-84` (used by S3 reconciliation).

**UploadReference contract:** `upload_reference.rb:3` — `belongs_to :upload`, polymorphic `:target`; `ensure_exist!` (`upload_reference.rb:9`) is the bulk reconcile entry used by every non-post dependent.

---

## Step 3 — How an upload is created and processed

1. **Creation entry point — `UploadCreator#create_for`** — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59`. Runs **inline** (synchronous, request thread or job). Validates, dedups by sha1 (`upload_creator.rb:132-148`), image-processes (HEIF→JPEG, SVG clean, favicon convert, orientation, crop, optimize, downsize — `upload_creator.rb:91-117`), computes sha1/secure hash (`upload_creator.rb:119-124`), builds the `Upload` row (`upload_creator.rb:166-217`), sets secure flags via `UploadSecurity` (`upload_creator.rb:219-224`), fires `DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, ...)` (`upload_creator.rb:232`), then saves.

2. **Storage — `Discourse.store.store_upload`** — inline, `upload_creator.rb:259`. `BaseStore#store_upload` computes path via `get_path_for_upload` (`base_store.rb:12,124`) and writes (LocalStore or S3Store override). External-upload path uses `move_existing_stored_upload` (`upload_creator.rb:252`). URL written back at `upload_creator.rb:266-267`.

3. **Inline post-creation fan-out:**
   - Avatar thumbnails enqueued — `Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, ...)` — `upload_creator.rb:276` (**async**).
   - `UserUpload.find_or_create_by!` — `upload_creator.rb:280` (inline).
   - `calculate_dominant_color!` — inline during build, `upload_creator.rb:214` (model method at `upload.rb:377`).

4. **Variant generation:**
   - `OptimizedImage.create_for` — `optimized_image.rb:35`. Runs **inline where called** (e.g. thumbnails on demand from `upload.rb:162`), guarded by a `DistributedMutex` per machine (`optimized_image.rb:11-24`); downloads original via `Discourse.store.download` if not local (`optimized_image.rb:66-71`).
   - Video conversion — `after_create` enqueues `:convert_video` (`upload.rb:690`) → **async** job, status-checked by `Jobs::CheckVideoConversionStatus`.

5. **Post processing (cooking, when an upload is referenced in a post):**
   - `CookedPostProcessor` / `CookedProcessorMixin` — `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:3`. Runs **async** (via `Jobs::ProcessPost`/`rebake!`). Links uploads via `HasPostUploadReferences#link_post_uploads` (`has_post_upload_references.rb:6`).
   - Hotlinked image pulling — `Jobs::PullHotlinkedImages` (`app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb`) — **async**.

6. **Remote storage / async upkeep:**
   - `Jobs::SyncAccessControlForUploads` (`app/jobs/regular/sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb`) — **async** ACL sync.
   - `Jobs::MigrateUploadScheme` (`app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb`) → `Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme` (`upload.rb:533`) — **async/scheduled**.
   - `Jobs::UpdateAnimatedUploads`, `Jobs::PurgeDeletedUploads`, `Jobs::EnsureS3UploadsExistence` — **scheduled**.

---

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Upload contract

Grouped by area. Each is an individual dependent.

### A. Post rendering / cooking
- `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:6` — `link_post_uploads` resolves URLs→Upload, writes `UploadReference` rows, sets `access_control_post_id`.
- `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:56` — `each_upload_url` parses cooked HTML for upload URLs.
- `app/models/post.rb:13` — `include HasPostUploadReferences`.
- `app/models/post.rb:43-44` — `has_many :upload_references` / `:uploads`.
- `app/models/post.rb:68` — `belongs_to :image_upload` (post thumbnail).
- `app/models/post.rb:339-350` — `each_upload_url` rewrites secure URLs in `should_secure_uploads?` flow.
- `app/models/post.rb:1094` — `update_uploads_secure_status`.
- `app/models/post.rb:848` — destroys failed `post_hotlinked_media`.
- `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb` — image dimensions/lightbox/optimized image generation during cook.
- `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:3` — shared cooking logic (`Discourse.store` usage, hotlinked image processing).
- `lib/pretty_text.rb` — uses `Discourse.store` to resolve upload URLs during cook.
- `lib/pretty_text/helpers.rb` — upload short-URL lookup during rendering.

## Step 5 — How upload identity and storage location are guarded

**Identity / "is this a real, current upload" guards:**
- `Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` — `upload.rb:86` — canonical resolver: sha1 first, else `get_from_url`.
- `HasUrl#get_from_url` — `concerns/has_url.rb:21-47` — parses URL, extracts sha1 via `URL_REGEX`, validates `sha1.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH` (`has_url.rb:43`), falls back to `find_by url LIKE`.
- `HasUrl.extract_sha1` — `has_url.rb:11-19` — rejects if sha1 length ≠ 40.
- sha1 decoders: `Upload.sha1_from_short_url/short_path/long_url/base62_encoded` — `upload.rb:457-478` (`base62_encoded` rejects over-length, `:470`).
- `Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(url)` — `local_store.rb:24` / `s3_store.rb:142` — confirms the URL belongs to this store before treating it as an upload (used in `has_post_upload_references.rb:123`).
- `verification_status` enum + scopes — `upload.rb:68-84` — S3 reconciliation marks `invalid_etag` / `s3_file_missing_confirmed`; `S3Inventory` and `EnsureS3UploadsExistence` act on these.
- `UploadRecovery#ensure_upload!` / `valid_sha1?` — `upload_recovery.rb:67,182` — confirms sha1 well-formed and file exists before recreating.
- `OptimizedImage.create_for` guards — `optimized_image.rb:36-37` (`return if upload.sha1.blank?`), version mismatch destroy-and-regenerate (`:56-59`).

**Authorization guard before serving:**
- `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#check_secure_upload_permission` — `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17-24` — uses `upload.access_control_post_id` + `guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)`; the unscoped `access_control_post` (`upload.rb:22`) ensures deleted posts still gate access.
- `upload_from_path_and_extension` / `upload_from_full_url` — `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6,13` — resolve by sha1 (strips optimized `_` suffix).
- `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` — `upload_security.rb:75-83` — the full insecure/secure check chain, with `access_control_post` as the priority signal (`:115-117,180-182`).

**Storage location / URL resolvers:**
- `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` — `base_store.rb:124-140` — prefers extracting path from `upload.url` via `UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX`, else computes from `id`+`sha1`+`extension`.
- `BaseStore#get_path_for_optimized_image` — `base_store.rb:142-154`.
- `BaseStore#path_for` (overridden in `local_store.rb:57`, `s3_store.rb:213`), `download`/`download!` (`base_store.rb:94-113`) with per-sha1 `DistributedMutex` (`base_store.rb:185`), `perform_download` chooses signed vs cdn URL based on `secure?` (`base_store.rb:196-201`).
- `S3Store#url_for`, `cdn_url`, `signed_url_for_path` — `s3_store.rb:218,232,242`; `LocalStore#cdn_url` — `local_store.rb:53`.
- `GlobalPath#upload_cdn_path` / `full_cdn_url` — `global_path.rb:12,26`.
- `Upload#local?` — `upload.rb:291` — decides path-vs-download resolution (used in email, dimensions, dominant color).
- Secure-URL ↔ upload-URL conversion: `Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url`, `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` — `upload.rb:259-275`.
- Backup remapping of storage location: `BackupRestore::UploadsRestorer#remap_uploads` — `uploads_restorer.rb:88-115`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

You are changing three things: **(a) how an upload stores, (b) how it resolves its URL, (c) how it tears down derived data.** Ranked by risk:

**🔴 Critical (silent data loss or broken access control):**
1. `Upload#destroy` + `before_destroy`/`after_destroy` nulling — `upload.rb:53-64,212-217`. Changing teardown can orphan or wrongly null `UserProfile`, `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar`, `Badge` (`:34`). Any new FK owner not added here leaks. **Re-verify every `belongs_to ... class_name: "Upload"` owner (Step 4 G/H) is covered.**
2. `dependent: :destroy` cascades — `optimized_images`, `optimized_videos`, `user_uploads`, `upload_references`, `post_hotlinked_media` (`upload.rb:26-31`). A storage-teardown change must still call `store.remove_upload` for each variant or files leak in S3.
3. `access_control_post` unscoped override + `check_secure_upload_permission` — `upload.rb:22`, `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17`. A URL/identity change that breaks sha1 resolution could **fail open** (serve secure files) or fail closed (break all media).
4. `UploadSecurity` / `update_secure_status` / `sync_optimized_videos_secure_status` — `upload_security.rb`, `upload.rb:492-523`. URL/ACL changes must keep S3 ACL in sync; `TopicUploadSecurityManager` (`topic_upload_security_manager.rb:18`) and `update_post_uploads_secure_status` job depend on it.

**🟠 High (broken rendering / references):**
5. `HasUrl#get_from_url` + `URL_REGEX` — `has_url.rb:21`, `upload.rb:11`. If URL format changes, every resolver (`fetch_from`, `each_upload_url`, `upload_recovery`, secure endpoints, `inline_uploads`) misses uploads → broken images, dropped references.
6. `HasPostUploadReferences#each_upload_url`/`link_post_uploads` — `has_post_upload_references.rb:6,56`. Cooked-HTML URL parsing is pattern-coupled to the URL scheme; a URL change orphans `UploadReference` rows → `clean_up_uploads` (`clean_up_uploads.rb:11`) then deletes "unused" uploads. **High chance of silent deletion.**
7. `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` URL-extraction (`base_store.rb:124-140`) and `get_path_for_optimized_image` (`:142`). Path derived from URL — a URL-format change can desync path computation → `remove_upload`/`path_for`/`download` hit wrong keys.
8. `S3Inventory` / `EnsureS3UploadsExistence` / `verification_status` — `s3_inventory.rb`, `upload.rb:68-84`. Storage/URL change can mass-mark valid uploads as missing.

**🟡 Medium (feature-specific):**
9. Email attachment resolution — `email/sender.rb:364-412` (path_for/download!/sha1 map).
10. Backup restore remap — `uploads_restorer.rb:88` (URL base remapping breaks if URL scheme changes).
11. Theme/site-setting/avatar serializers and `Discourse.store` consumers (Step 4 F/G/I) — broken asset URLs.
12. `migrate_to_new_scheme` — `upload.rb:533` (already does URL remap; overlaps with your rework — reconcile).
13. Plugin hooks — `add_unused_callback`/`add_in_use_callback` (`upload.rb:96`), `before_upload_creation` event, `register_custom_public_type`. External plugins (e.g. `discourse-ai`) depend on these.

**Must re-verify after the change:** the full owner list in Step 4 G/H; `clean_up_uploads` orphan logic; S3 ACL sync; secure endpoint access control; email attachments; backup/restore round-trip; rebake of existing posts (URL changes need `rebake_posts_on_old_scheme`, `upload.rb:488`).

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Discourse — a large open-source forum platform. Rails monolith backend (`app/`, `lib/`) with an Ember.js frontend (`frontend/discourse/app`), Sidekiq background jobs (`app/jobs`), and a plugin system (`plugins/`).

**Layout that matters for this ticket:**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models, including the upload contract.
- `app/models/concerns/` — shared mixins included into models.
- `lib/file_store/` — the storage abstraction (local vs S3).
- `lib/` — service-like helpers (`upload_creator.rb`, `upload_security.rb`, `upload_recovery.rb`, `cooked_post_processor.rb`, `s3_inventory.rb`).
- `app/jobs/regular` + `app/jobs/scheduled` — async processing/cleanup.
- `lib/backup_restore/` — backup + restore.
- `app/services/` — service objects.

**The model representing an uploaded file:** `app/models/upload.rb:5` (`class Upload < ActiveRecord::Base`). The DB schema is annotated at `app/models/upload.rb:695-736` (key columns: `url`, `sha1`, `original_sha1`, `extension`, `secure`, `access_control_post_id`, `verification_status`).

**Shared pieces it is built on:**
- Concern `HasUrl` — `app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3` (URL→record resolution by sha1/url).
- Join model `UploadReference` — `app/models/upload_reference.rb:3` (polymorphic glue between an upload and posts/themes/etc.).
- Concern `HasPostUploadReferences` — `app/models/concerns/has_post_upload_references.rb:3` (extracts upload URLs from cooked HTML and links them).
- Storage abstraction `FileStore::BaseStore` — `lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7`, with `LocalStore` (`lib/file_store/local_store.rb`) and `S3Store` (`lib/file_store/s3_store.rb`), reached via `Discourse.store`.
- Derived records: `OptimizedImage` (`app/models/optimized_image.rb:3`) and `OptimizedVideo` (`app/models/optimized_video.rb`).

---

## Step 2 — The Upload contract and what it is built on

**The model:** `app/models/upload.rb:5`.

**Shared behavior it pulls in:**
- `include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper` — `upload.rb:6` (used by `human_filesize`, `upload.rb:484`).
- `include HasUrl` — `upload.rb:7` → concern at `concerns/has_url.rb:3`. Provides class methods `extract_url`, `extract_sha1`, `get_from_url`, `get_from_urls` keyed off `URL_REGEX` (`upload.rb:11`). This is the contract other code uses to turn a URL/path back into an Upload.

**Associations other records hang off it:**
- `belongs_to :user` — `upload.rb:17`; `belongs_to :access_control_post` — `upload.rb:18` (overridden unscoped at `upload.rb:22` so deleted posts still resolve — security-critical).
- `has_many :post_hotlinked_media` — `upload.rb:26`.
- `has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy` — `upload.rb:27` (variants).
- `has_many :optimized_videos` + `:optimized_video_uploads` through them — `upload.rb:28-29` (variants).
- `has_many :user_uploads` — `upload.rb:30`.
- `has_many :upload_references` + `has_many :posts, through:` — `upload.rb:31-32` (the polymorphic backbone).
- `has_many :topic_thumbnails` — `upload.rb:33`.
- `has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify` — `upload.rb:34`.

**Variant/derived spawning:**
- `after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job` — `upload.rb:35,690` → enqueues `Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, ...)`.
- `get_optimized_image` / `create_thumbnail!` — `upload.rb:147-172` → `OptimizedImage.create_for`.

**Teardown contract (the part being reworked):**
- `before_destroy` nulls `UserProfile.card_background_upload_id`/`profile_background_upload_id` — `upload.rb:53-58`.
- `after_destroy` nulls `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar.gravatar_upload_id`/`custom_upload_id` — `upload.rb:60-64`.
- `destroy` override wraps `Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)` + `super` in a transaction — `upload.rb:212-217`. **This is the teardown seam.**

**URL/identity contract surface a teammate must know:**
- `short_url` / `short_path` / `base62_sha1` — `upload.rb:219-289`.

**UploadReference contract:** `upload_reference.rb:3` — `belongs_to :upload`, polymorphic `:target`; `ensure_exist!` (`upload_reference.rb:9`) is the bulk reconcile entry used by every non-post dependent.

## Step 3 — How an upload is created and processed

1. **Creation entry point — `UploadCreator#create_for`** — `lib/upload_creator.rb:59`. Runs **inline** (synchronous, request thread or job). Validates, dedups by sha1 (`upload_creator.rb:132-148`), image-processes (HEIF→JPEG, SVG clean, favicon convert, orientation, crop, optimize, downsize — `upload_creator.rb:91-117`), computes sha1/secure hash (`upload_creator.rb:119-124`), builds the `Upload` row (`upload_creator.rb:166-217`), sets secure flags via `UploadSecurity` (`upload_creator.rb:219-224`), fires `DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, ...)` (`upload_creator.rb:232`), then saves.

2. **Storage — `Discourse.store.store_upload`** — inline, `upload_creator.rb:259`. `BaseStore#store_upload` computes path via `get_path_for_upload` (`base_store.rb:12,124`) and writes (LocalStore or S3Store override). External-upload path uses `move_existing_stored_upload` (`upload_creator.rb:252`). URL written back at `upload_creator.rb:266-267`.

3. **Inline post-creation fan-out:**
   - Avatar thumbnails enqueued — `Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, ...)` — `upload_creator.rb:276` (**async**).
   - `UserUpload.find_or_create_by!` — `upload_creator.rb:280` (inline).
   - `calculate_dominant_color!` — inline during build, `upload_creator.rb:214` (model method at `upload.rb:377`).

4. **Variant generation:**
   - `OptimizedImage.create_for` — `optimized_image.rb:35`. Runs **inline where called** (e.g. thumbnails on demand from `upload.rb:162`), guarded by a `DistributedMutex` per machine (`optimized_image.rb:11-24`); downloads original via `Discourse.store.download` if not local (`optimized_image.rb:66-71`).
   - Video conversion — `after_create` enqueues `:convert_video` (`upload.rb:690`) → **async** job, status-checked by `Jobs::CheckVideoConversionStatus`.

5. **Post processing (cooking, when an upload is referenced in a post):**
   - `CookedPostProcessor` / `CookedProcessorMixin` — `lib/cooked_post_processor.rb`, `lib/cooked_processor_mixin.rb:3`. Runs **async** (via `Jobs::ProcessPost`/`rebake!`). Links uploads via `HasPostUploadReferences#link_post_uploads` (`has_post_upload_references.rb:6`).
   - Hotlinked image pulling — `Jobs::PullHotlinkedImages` (`app/jobs/regular/pull_hotlinked_images.rb`) — **async**.

6. **Remote storage / async upkeep:**
   - `Jobs::SyncAccessControlForUploads` (`app/jobs/regular/sync_access_control_for_uploads.rb`) — **async** ACL sync.
   - `Jobs::MigrateUploadScheme` (`app/jobs/scheduled/migrate_upload_scheme.rb`) → `Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme` (`upload.rb:533`) — **async/scheduled**.
   - `Jobs::UpdateAnimatedUploads`, `Jobs::PurgeDeletedUploads`, `Jobs::EnsureS3UploadsExistence` — **scheduled**.

## Step 5 — How upload identity and storage location are guarded

**Identity / "is this a real, current upload" guards:**
- `Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)` — `upload.rb:86` — canonical resolver: sha1 first, else `get_from_url`.
- `HasUrl#get_from_url` — `concerns/has_url.rb:21-47` — parses URL, extracts sha1 via `URL_REGEX`, validates `sha1.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH` (`has_url.rb:43`), falls back to `find_by url LIKE`.
- `HasUrl.extract_sha1` — `has_url.rb:11-19` — rejects if sha1 length ≠ 40.
- sha1 decoders: `Upload.sha1_from_short_url/short_path/long_url/base62_encoded` — `upload.rb:457-478` (`base62_encoded` rejects over-length, `:470`).
- `Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(url)` — `local_store.rb:24` / `s3_store.rb:142` — confirms the URL belongs to this store before treating it as an upload (used in `has_post_upload_references.rb:123`).
- `verification_status` enum + scopes — `upload.rb:68-84` — S3 reconciliation marks `invalid_etag` / `s3_file_missing_confirmed`; `S3Inventory` and `EnsureS3UploadsExistence` act on these.
- `UploadRecovery#ensure_upload!` / `valid_sha1?` — `upload_recovery.rb:67,182` — confirms sha1 well-formed and file exists before recreating.
- `OptimizedImage.create_for` guards — `optimized_image.rb:36-37` (`return if upload.sha1.blank?`), version mismatch destroy-and-regenerate (`:56-59`).

**Authorization guard before serving:**
- `SecureUploadEndpointHelpers#check_secure_upload_permission` — `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17-24` — uses `upload.access_control_post_id` + `guardian.can_see?(upload.access_control_post)`; the unscoped `access_control_post` (`upload.rb:22`) ensures deleted posts still gate access.
- `upload_from_path_and_extension` / `upload_from_full_url` — `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:6,13` — resolve by sha1 (strips optimized `_` suffix).
- `UploadSecurity#should_be_secure_with_reason` — `upload_security.rb:75-83` — the full insecure/secure check chain, with `access_control_post` as the priority signal (`:115-117,180-182`).

**Storage location / URL resolvers:**
- `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` — `base_store.rb:124-140` — prefers extracting path from `upload.url` via `UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX`, else computes from `id`+`sha1`+`extension`.
- `BaseStore#get_path_for_optimized_image` — `base_store.rb:142-154`.
- `BaseStore#path_for` (overridden in `local_store.rb:57`, `s3_store.rb:213`), `download`/`download!` (`base_store.rb:94-113`) with per-sha1 `DistributedMutex` (`base_store.rb:185`), `perform_download` chooses signed vs cdn URL based on `secure?` (`base_store.rb:196-201`).
- `S3Store#url_for`, `cdn_url`, `signed_url_for_path` — `s3_store.rb:218,232,242`; `LocalStore#cdn_url` — `local_store.rb:53`.
- `GlobalPath#upload_cdn_path` / `full_cdn_url` — `global_path.rb:12,26`.
- `Upload#local?` — `upload.rb:291` — decides path-vs-download resolution (used in email, dimensions, dominant color).
- Secure-URL ↔ upload-URL conversion: `Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url`, `signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url` — `upload.rb:259-275`.
- Backup remapping of storage location: `BackupRestore::UploadsRestorer#remap_uploads` — `uploads_restorer.rb:88-115`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change

You are changing three things: **(a) how an upload stores, (b) how it resolves its URL, (c) how it tears down derived data.** Ranked by risk:

**🔴 Critical (silent data loss or broken access control):**
1. `Upload#destroy` + `before_destroy`/`after_destroy` nulling — `upload.rb:53-64,212-217`. Changing teardown can orphan or wrongly null `UserProfile`, `User.uploaded_avatar_id`, `UserAvatar`, `Badge` (`:34`). Any new FK owner not added here leaks. **Re-verify every `belongs_to ... class_name: "Upload"` owner (Step 4 G/H) is covered.**
2. `dependent: :destroy` cascades — `optimized_images`, `optimized_videos`, `user_uploads`, `upload_references`, `post_hotlinked_media` (`upload.rb:26-31`). A storage-teardown change must still call `store.remove_upload` for each variant or files leak in S3.
3. `access_control_post` unscoped override + `check_secure_upload_permission` — `upload.rb:22`, `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17`. A URL/identity change that breaks sha1 resolution could **fail open** (serve secure files) or fail closed (break all media).
4. `UploadSecurity` / `update_secure_status` / `sync_optimized_videos_secure_status` — `upload_security.rb`, `upload.rb:492-523`. URL/ACL changes must keep S3 ACL in sync; `TopicUploadSecurityManager` (`topic_upload_security_manager.rb:18`) and `update_post_uploads_secure_status` job depend on it.

**🟠 High (broken rendering / references):**
5. `HasUrl#get_from_url` + `URL_REGEX` — `has_url.rb:21`, `upload.rb:11`. If URL format changes, every resolver (`fetch_from`, `each_upload_url`, `upload_recovery`, secure endpoints, `inline_uploads`) misses uploads → broken images, dropped references.
6. `HasPostUploadReferences#each_upload_url`/`link_post_uploads` — `has_post_upload_references.rb:6,56`. Cooked-HTML URL parsing is pattern-coupled to the URL scheme; a URL change orphans `UploadReference` rows → `clean_up_uploads` (`clean_up_uploads.rb:11`) then deletes "unused" uploads. **High chance of silent deletion.**
7. `BaseStore#get_path_for_upload` URL-extraction (`base_store.rb:124-140`) and `get_path_for_optimized_image` (`:142`). Path derived from URL — a URL-format change can desync path computation → `remove_upload`/`path_for`/`download` hit wrong keys.
8. `S3Inventory` / `EnsureS3UploadsExistence` / `verification_status` — `s3_inventory.rb`, `upload.rb:68-84`. Storage/URL change can mass-mark valid uploads as missing.

**🟡 Medium (feature-specific):**
9. Email attachment resolution — `email/sender.rb:364-412` (path_for/download!/sha1 map).
10. Backup restore remap — `uploads_restorer.rb:88` (URL base remapping breaks if URL scheme changes).
11. Theme/site-setting/avatar serializers and `Discourse.store` consumers (Step 4 F/G/I) — broken asset URLs.
12. `migrate_to_new_scheme` — `upload.rb:533` (already does URL remap; overlaps with your rework — reconcile).
13. Plugin hooks — `add_unused_callback`/`add_in_use_callback` (`upload.rb:96`), `before_upload_creation` event, `register_custom_public_type`. External plugins (e.g. `discourse-ai`) depend on these.

**Must re-verify after the change:** the full owner list in Step 4 G/H; `clean_up_uploads` orphan logic; S3 ACL sync; secure endpoint access control; email attachments; backup/restore round-trip; rebake of existing posts (URL changes need `rebake_posts_on_old_scheme`, `upload.rb:488`).
